summary: The diff implements all three requirements (REQ-36, REQ-37, REQ-38) with mostly complete coverage. However, there are a few gaps: the backend stable var uses var (non-stable), the getMessages parameter type uses Time.Time (Int) instead of Nat as specified, and not all message creation points are shown to assign a unique id. The core functionality — backend message store, polling, BroadcastChannel preservation, deduplication, and id field — is present.

missing_requirements.requirement: backend/main.mo defines a stable var for chat message storage
missing_requirements.reason: The diff shows 'var messages : [ChatMessage] = []' without the 'stable' keyword, meaning message history will not survive canister upgrades.

missing_requirements.requirement: getMessages query accepts a since: Nat timestamp
missing_requirements.reason: The diff declares getMessages(since : Time.Time) where Time.Time is Int (nanoseconds), not Nat as specified in the requirement.

missing_requirements.requirement: Every message sent (user, bot, admin, system) has a unique non-empty id assigned at creation time
missing_requirements.reason: The diff does not show changes to message creation points (ChatPage, admin broadcast, force text, bot responses) to ensure a unique id is assigned at creation. The frontend-file-summaries.txt does not indicate these files were modified to add id generation.

hallucinated_features.description: postMessage overwrites the incoming message's id with an auto-incremented integer id (nextId.toText()) instead of preserving the client-supplied id
hallucinated_features.reason: The requirement states the id is generated on the frontend (UUID or timestamp+random string) and used for deduplication. The backend replacing it with a server-generated integer breaks the deduplication contract between BroadcastChannel and polling.
